rationale: The downstream selection/result contract is otherwise unchanged: Bead 3 still dispatches by the 109 runner key, workflow mode still invokes /ms-panel once with the same args, and the workflow result remains verbatim panel verify plus panel tally stdout. The destructured command constants and target grammar are intended as Bead 2 internals. However, the round-2 dash guard regresses that internal path: buildCommand is specified to reject any arg starting with '-', while the prescribed call sites pass fixed option tokens such as --spec and --target through args. Implemented literally, registration cannot run, so no consumed verify/tally result is produced.
concrete_changes_required: In Bead 2, reconcile buildCommand's leading-dash guard with fixed CLI option tokens: either make buildCommand accept structured fixed flags outside the guarded user-value list, or state that the guard applies only to untrusted/user-derived values, then update the examples/tests accordingly.

findings.title: Leading-dash guard rejects prescribed fixed flags
findings.detail: Plan lines 446-451 say buildCommand rejects any args element beginning with '-', but lines 477-478 prescribe buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE, slug, "--spec", spec, "--target", target, ...). Those fixed flags would be rejected before panel create runs, preventing the workflow from returning the unchanged verify/tally result.
